findings.evidence: Grep: `LaplacianLocalExtr.lean:109: theorem deriv_deriv_comp_line {f : E → ℝ} ...`; Mathlib’s surrounding Laplacian API is vector-valued, e.g. `Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Laplacian.lean:175: theorem laplacian_eq_iteratedFDeriv_orthonormalBasis ...` under variables `{F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ℝ F] ...`.
findings.fix: Generalize `deriv_deriv_comp_line` (and any private helper it needs) to vector-valued codomain `F`, using `ContinuousLinearMap.apply ℝ F w`; keep the later sign lemmas specialized to `ℝ`.
findings.issue: `deriv_deriv_comp_line` is unnecessarily specialized to `f : E → ℝ`; the statement is a pure calculus identity and should work for `f : E → F` with `[NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ℝ F]`.

summary: One public calculus lemma is stated below the natural Mathlib level: the line/Hessian identity is scalar-only even though the proof and surrounding Laplacian API are vector-valued.
